app.title: Wolfcha | AI Werewolf - Play Werewolf with AI Online
app.description: Play Werewolf with AI opponents online. The ultimate single-player social deduction game featuring DeepSeek, Qwen, Kimi & Gemini AI models with voice acting.

springCampaign.signInHint: Sign in to automatically claim 10 Spring quota for today.

modal.title: Happy Lunar New Year
modal.subtitle: Wishing you luck and great reads in every match.
modal.line1: During the event, sign in daily to receive 10 Spring quota. They are consumed first when a match starts.
modal.line2: We also want to be honest: after the Lunar New Year maintenance window, we may have to pause Wolfcha due to operational pressure.
modal.line3: Thank you for being with us all this way. We hope Wolfcha has kept you company through some joyful and relaxing moments.
modal.note: Event window: Feb 12 - Feb 17 (Asia/Shanghai, ends at 00:00 on Feb 18)

claimed.title: Lunar New Year bonus claimed
claimed.description: You received +10 Spring quota for today.

productHunt.title: Vote for Wolfcha on Product Hunt
productHunt.description: If you enjoyed this game, an upvote helps a lot and takes only a moment.

sponsor.title: Become a sponsor
sponsor.subtitle: Your support keeps Wolfcha running smoothly and free for everyone.
sponsor.description: Wolfcha is a community-driven AI Werewolf project. Our costs go toward AI models, voice synthesis, servers, and content creation. Any support—big or small—means the world to us.

items.media: Voice, sound effects, character art assets

sponsor.note: Sponsors are featured on our homepage with a seal. Anonymous contributions are also welcome.

cards.openCreator: OpenCreator helps us generate all night-time character art
cards.bailian: Dashscope provides us with AI model capabilities
cards.zenmux: Aggregating top global AI models to power every strategic showdown.

sponsor.mailBody:
Hi, I'm interested in supporting Wolfcha and would like to offer help in the following ways:

- (Model credits / compute)
- (Voice / SFX assets)
- (Design / product collaboration)
- (Other)

My contact info:

oath.line1: I willingly enter this game of deception and truth.
oath.line2: When darkness falls, I shall conceal my identity;
oath.line3: When dawn breaks, I shall judge the guilty.

hero.title: AI Werewolf
hero.subtitle: Play with AI Opponents
hero.description: Experience the classic social deduction game like never before. Battle against multiple AI models that reason, bluff, and strategize — all in a single-player experience.

solo.title: Play Alone, Anytime
solo.description: No need to gather friends. Play a full Werewolf game solo with AI opponents that reason, bluff, and strategize.

models.title: Multiple AI Models
models.description: Each AI player is powered by a different language model, creating diverse personalities and play styles.

voice.title: Voice Acting
voice.description: Immersive experience with AI-generated voice acting for every character.

roles.title: Classic Roles
roles.description: All your favorite roles: Werewolf, Seer, Witch, Hunter, Guard, and Villager.

models.title: Powered by Top AI Models
models.description: Each AI player uses a different language model, creating unique personalities and strategies

step1.title: Join the Game
step1.description: Enter your name and choose your settings. AI players join automatically.

step2.title: Play Your Role
step2.description: Use your abilities at night. Discuss and vote during the day.

step3.title: Win Together or Alone
step3.description: Eliminate all werewolves to win as a villager, or dominate as a wolf.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: AI Werewolf is a single-player version of the classic social deduction game where you play against AI opponents powered by advanced language models.
faq.q2: Can I really play Werewolf alone?
faq.a2: Yes! All other players are AI-controlled, each with unique personalities and reasoning abilities. It feels like playing with real people.
faq.q3: Which AI models power the game?
faq.a3: We use DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen3-235B, Kimi K2, Gemini 3 Flash, and ByteDance Seed 1.8. Each brings different strengths to the game.
faq.a4: Yes, Wolfcha is free to play. Start immediately at wolf-cha.com.
faq.a5: Classic roles include Werewolf, Seer, Witch, Hunter, Guard, and Villager. Role composition varies by player count (8-12).

cta.title: Ready to Play AI Werewolf?
cta.description: No signup required. Start playing for free right now.

tutorialModal.title: Beginner Guide: How to Play Werewolf
tutorialModal.intro: This is a faction game of hidden roles, reasoning, and table talk. Follow the system prompts and act or speak when it's your turn.

factions.title: 1. Factions and Win Conditions
factions.line1: Good side: Villager, Seer, Witch, Hunter, Guard. Win by banishing all werewolves.
factions.line2: Wolf side: Werewolves. Win when good players can no longer oppose them (usually wolves ≥ alive goods).

flow.title: 2. Game Flow (Night → Day Cycle)
flow.line1: Night: roles act in order (Guard/Werewolf/Witch/Seer, etc.).
flow.line2: Day: reveal the night outcome, speak in order, then vote to banish.
flow.line3: Special: if the Hunter dies or is banished, they may shoot one player.

speech.title: 3. Speaking and Voting (Tips)
speech.line1: Listen first: watch for contradictions and logic gaps.
speech.line2: Explain clearly: why you suspect or trust someone (based on what was said).
speech.line3: Review before voting: who seems least consistent or is pushing the narrative.

werewolf.title: Werewolf
werewolf.description: Choose a player to kill at night. Werewolves are teammates and may coordinate. Hide your identity by day.

seer.title: Seer
seer.description: Check one player each night to learn if they're a wolf or good. Build credibility during the day.

witch.title: Witch
witch.description: Has one antidote and one poison. Antidote saves last night's victim; poison kills a player. Use with good timing.

hunter.title: Hunter
hunter.description: If killed or banished, may shoot one player. Leave clear final info before dying.

guard.title: Guard
guard.description: Protect one player at night from a wolf kill (exact rules follow prompts). Often protects key roles.

villager.title: Villager
villager.description: No night skills, but daytime speech and votes are crucial. Use logic and information to find wolves.

tips.title: 5. Quick Tips
tips.line1: If you're a wolf: blend in, build reasonable suspicion chains, avoid collapsing the discussion.
tips.line2: If you're good: avoid emotional votes; use verifiable info to reason.

werewolf.title: Werewolf
werewolf.subtitle: Hunt at night, disguise by day.
abilities: Coordinate with teammates at night to choose a target
Disguise as a good player during the day
tips: Coordinate with your team, avoid collapsing the night talk
Don't be too perfect in daytime speech

seer.title: Seer
seer.subtitle: You see the truth, but must earn trust.
abilities: Guide votes with verified info by day

witch.title: Witch
witch.subtitle: Life and death hang on a single choice.
tips: Potions are precious; don't be dragged by tempo

hunter.title: Hunter
hunter.subtitle: When you fall, take one with you.
abilities: If banished or killed, you can take one player with you
tips: Choose your target before you fall

guard.title: Guard
guard.subtitle: Shield the right person at the right moment.
abilities: Protect one player at night from attacks
Usually cannot guard the same player on consecutive nights

villager.title: Villager
villager.subtitle: No powers, but your judgment matters.
abilities: Find wolves through speech and voting

idiot.title: Idiot
idiot.subtitle: Immune to exile once — flip your card and survive.
abilities: When voted out, auto-reveal and survive once
After revealing, lose voting rights but can still speak
tips: Speak boldly — you can't be voted out easily
After revealing, influence others through speech

whiteWolfKing.title: White Wolf King
whiteWolfKing.subtitle: King of wolves — self-destruct to take someone down.
abilities: At night, coordinate with wolf team to kill
During the day, you can self-destruct to eliminate one player

speakOrder.first: You speak first. No one has spoken yet.
speakOrder.last: You are the last speaker ({speakOrder}/{totalSpeakers}). Everyone has spoken. Don't say things like "let's see what Seat X says".
speakOrder.middle: You are speaker {speakOrder}/{totalSpeakers}. Already spoken: {spokenList}. Yet to speak: {unspokenList}. Note: players who have already spoken will NOT speak again—do not question them, ask for clarification, or say things like "I'd like to hear Seat X's view"; only analyze their statements.

campaign.badge:
【Sheriff Badge】
The sheriff has 1.5 votes and can transfer the badge upon death. The sheriff leads discussions and consolidates votes.

【Campaign requirements】
1. Explain why you're running (What info do you have? Why are you fit to lead?)
2. Provide a leadership commitment (Who to watch, how to vote, how to handle counter-claims)
3. No empty slogans - give concrete analysis or plans
campaign.pk: 【PK requirements】Explain why your opponent is less suitable or why you're the better choice. Include your leadership commitment or focus.
campaign.candidateNote: Note: Only candidates speak in this phase. Non-candidates ({list}) will not speak.
campaign.emptyCandidateNote: Note: Candidate list is empty (unusual). Only respond based on visible speech records; do not speculate about non-speakers.

roleHints.werewolf: You are a werewolf. Blend in as a villager; deflect suspicion subtly without overdoing it. If you are running for sheriff, in most cases you should hard-claim Seer in your campaign speech — fabricate a check result, announce badge-flow intent, and give a vote direction.
roleHints.seer: You are the Seer. After running for sheriff, almost always reveal your identity immediately. Your campaign speech should include: your check result, a vote commitment (if wolf: name the target; if good: commit to deciding based on today's speeches), and your badge-flow plan (who you check tonight / who gets the badge if you die).

task.lastWords:
You've been eliminated. Deliver your last words.
【Identity Check】You are Seat #{seat} ({name}). Do not confuse your seat number.
task.lastWordsWerewolfStrategy:
【Last Words Strategy】
As a werewolf, your last words goals are:
1. Insist you are a good player; question whether the Seer's check is wrong or they are a fake-claiming wolf
2. Guide good players to focus on other suspicious players to relieve pressure on your wolf teammates
3. Do NOT admit you are a wolf, and do NOT directly accept the Seer's accusation
4. Hint at things like "If I were really a wolf, why would I XXX" to prove your innocence
5. Provide analysis of the game state, but from the perspective of a "wrongly accused good player"
task.campaign: Sheriff campaign speech. Deliver your campaign statement.

guidelines.genshin:
【Speaking rules】 1. Base judgments on current state and rules. Don't invent personas or catchphrases. 2. Keep it concise and explain your intent for this round. 3. Keep it around 4-6 sentences total; 1-2 sentences per bubble; keep each sentence short and punchy; total speech must not exceed {maxChars} characters. 4. Only discuss living players; avoid postmortems. 5. Timeline constraint: last night's kill is locked before today's daytime speeches. Do NOT treat today's campaign/claims/speeches as the direct cause of last night's kill. 6. **Never ask or expect a response from already-spoken players**: each player speaks only once per day and cannot respond. Analyze their statements; do NOT use phrases like "I'd like to hear what Seat X thinks" either. 7. **Language**: Speak in natural English only; do not insert random letter sequences, gibberish, or non-English characters. 8. **No tags or markup**: Output plain text only — no HTML tags (e.g. ``, `
`), no Markdown formatting (e.g. `**`, `##`), and no code blocks. 【Output format】 Return a JSON string array, each element a message bubble. Start your response directly with [, no text before or after the array.
guidelines.default:
【Core principles】 1. **In-game focus**: Speech must be based on actual game info. NEVER fabricate events. 2. **Only living players**: Only discuss current living players. 【Speaking guide】 - NEVER fabricate speeches! First speaker must not reference "earlier" speeches. - NEVER use ANY job-related expressions! No analogies (like selling insurance), jargon (from my report), or experiences (when I was a salesman). Use only Werewolf game terms. - Timeline constraint: last night's kill is locked before today's daytime speeches. Do NOT treat today's campaign/claims/speeches as the direct cause of last night's kill. - **Never ask or expect a response from already-spoken players**: each player speaks only once per day, so they cannot respond. You may analyze or challenge their statements, but do NOT use phrases like "Seat X, can you explain…", "Seat X, what do you say to that?", or "I'd like to hear what Seat X thinks". - 4-6 sentences, 1-2 per bubble, split into 2-5 bubbles; keep each sentence short and punchy; total speech must not exceed {maxChars} characters. - Use "Seat X" for players. No stage directions (e.g., *pushes glasses*). - **Language**: Speak in natural English only; do not insert gibberish, random letter sequences, or non-English characters. - **No tags or markup**: Output plain text only — no HTML tags (e.g. ``, `
`), no Markdown formatting (e.g. `**`, `##`), and no code blocks. {roleHintLine} 【Output format】 Return a JSON string array. Start your response directly with [, no text before or after the array.

phaseHint.badge: You are delivering a sheriff campaign speech. Follow campaign requirements strictly.
phaseHint.pk: You are delivering a sheriff PK speech. Follow PK requirements strictly.

roleHint.werewolf: Hint: avoid voting a wolf teammate, but don't be too obvious
roleHint.seer: Hint: decide based on your check result

vote.task:
【Voting Rules】
Each player votes to eliminate one suspect. Most votes = eliminated. Tie = PK speeches then revote.

【Voting Strategy】
- Stay consistent with your speech today
- Consider all speeches, not just recent ones
- Don't blindly follow sheriff's vote call - think independently
- If lacking evidence, vote for vague or flip-flopping players

【Task】
Choose one player to eliminate. Only output the seat number.

Options: {options}

{roleHints}
vote.user:
{gameContext}

【Today's discussion】
{todayTranscript}

【Your speech summary】
"{selfSpeech}"

Which seat do you vote?

【Format】
Reply with a seat number only, e.g., 3
No explanations, no extra text, no code blocks

seer.task:
【Seer Skill】
Each night you can check one player's identity (Werewolf/Good). Only you know the result; you can reveal or hide it.

【Check Strategy】
- First night: check edge or middle seats for basic info
- Later: check players with suspicious speech

【Task】
Choose one player to check. Only output the seat number.
{checkedLine}

Options: {options}
seer.user:
{context}

Which seat will you check?

【Format】
Reply with a seat number only, e.g., 5
No explanations, no extra text, no code blocks

wolf.solo: You are the only living werewolf
wolf.task:
【Werewolf Skill】
Each night wolves collectively decide to kill one player. Can kill good players, teammates (self-kill), or skip (no kill).

【Kill Strategy】
- Priority: Seer > Witch > Hunter > Guard
- Self-kill: the chosen wolf dies, creates confusion
- No kill: creates peaceful night to confuse good players

【Task】
Choose one player to kill. Only output the seat number.
{teammateVotesSection}

Options: {options}
wolf.user:
{context}

Which seat do you want to kill?

【Format】
Reply with a seat number only, e.g., 2
No explanations, no extra text, no code blocks

guard.lastTarget: You protected Seat {seat} last night; cannot pick again
guard.task:
【Guard Skill】
Each night you can protect one player from wolf kills. If successful, the target survives.

【Important Rules】
- Cannot protect the same player on consecutive nights
- Can protect yourself
- If guard and witch both save the same target (milk poison), they still die

【Guard Strategy】
- Prioritize protecting revealed roles (Seer, Witch)
- Also consider strong-speaking good players

【Task】
Choose one player to protect. Only output the seat number.

Options: {options}
{lastTargetLine}
guard.user:
{context}

Which seat do you want to protect?

【Format】
Reply with a seat number only, e.g., 3
No explanations, no extra text, no code blocks

witch.noSense: Antidote already used; no info on the target
witch.saveOption: - Input "save" to use antidote on Seat {seat}
witch.poisonOption: - Input "poison X" to poison Seat X (e.g., "poison 3")
witch.task:
【Witch Skill】
You have one antidote (save) and one poison (kill), each once per game.

【Important Rules】
- Antidote can save the wolf's victim (including yourself)
- Poison can kill any player (usually confirmed wolves)
- One potion max per night
- If guard and witch both save the same target (milk poison), they still die

【Potion Status】
Antidote: {healStatus} | Poison: {poisonStatus}

【Tonight】
{tonightInfo}

【Task】
Decide whether to use a potion. Only output commands.
{saveLine}
{poisonLine}
- Input "pass" to use no potion

Poison targets: {poisonTargets}
witch.user:
{context}

What will you do?

【Format】
Reply: save / poison X / pass
Only output the command itself, no explanation or extra text

signup.task:
【Sheriff Badge】
The sheriff has 1.5 votes and can transfer the badge upon death. The sheriff leads discussions and consolidates votes.

【Task】
This is the sheriff signup phase. Decide whether to run for sheriff.
- Good team: Running gives you leadership and vote control, but may make you a wolf target
- Wolf team: Running lets you mislead good players, but makes you more scrutinized

【Signup principle】If you have no important info to share, or cannot convince others or lead, prefer not to sign up (0).

【Output format】
Only output a single digit: 1 to sign up, 0 to skip
No explanations, no extra text, no code blocks
signup.wolfTacticJump:
【Special Tactic Order】
Suggested strategy this game: try fake-claiming Seer to fight for the badge.
The wolf team needs to create chaos and seize narrative control. You are the designated fake-claimer.
Prefer signing up (1) and pretend to be the real Seer on the badge stage; however, if the situation looks bad, you are allowed to stay off.
signup.wolfTacticSupport:
【Tactic Order】
Your teammate (Seat {seat}) may fake-claim Seer this game.
You can stay off this round and prepare to support (buddy / counter-push), but you may also sign up with a small chance to create noise depending on the situation.
Default tendency: choose not to sign up (0).
signup.wolfTacticObserve:
【Tactic Order】
Observe the situation this game. No need to rush for the badge.
Default tendency: choose not to sign up (0).

election.task:
【Sheriff Badge】
The sheriff has 1.5 votes and can transfer the badge upon death. Choose someone who can lead well.

【Task】
Sheriff election: choose a player to receive the badge.
- Good team: prioritize players with useful info, clear logic, and leadership ability
- Wolf team: prioritize wolf teammates or easily manipulated players
Consider overall performance, not just the last speech.
Only output the seat number. No analysis or roleplay.

Options: {options}
election.user:
{context}

Which seat do you vote for sheriff?

【Format】
Reply with a seat number only, e.g., 3
No explanations, no extra text, no code blocks

transfer.roleHintWerewolf: Hint: transfer to a wolf teammate or a controllable good player, or tear the badge
transfer.roleHintGood: Hint: transfer to the most trustworthy good player, or tear the badge
transfer.roleHintSeer: Hint (Seer): compare tonight's check result with the transfer target you announced during the day. If your announced target checked out as good, pass to them as planned. If they checked out as wolf, deliberately pass to a different good player instead — the deviation is the signal; other players will infer the announced target is a wolf. If you made no announcement or are not the sheriff, pass to your most trusted good player.
transfer.task:
【Task】
You are eliminated. Transfer the badge to a living player or tear it.
If transferring, choose the most trustworthy player.
If tearing, reply "0".

Options: {options}, or reply "0" to tear the badge

{roleHints}

【Format】
Reply with a seat number only, e.g., 3
No explanations, no extra text, no code blocks

hunter.task:
【Hunter Skill】
On death, you can shoot one player. Cannot shoot if poisoned.

【Shooting Strategy】
- Prioritize confirmed wolves
- If uncertain, you can pass (don't shoot)
- Shooting is immediate and irreversible

【Task】
Decide whether to shoot. Only output seat number or pass.

Options: {options}
hunter.user:
{context}

Which seat do you want to shoot?

【Format】
Reply with a seat number only, e.g., 5
If you don't want to shoot, reply: pass
No explanations, no extra text, no code blocks
hunter.lastWordsIntentHint: You stated in your last words that you would shoot Seat {seat} ({name}). Please execute your decision.
hunter.lastWordsPassHint: You stated in your last words that you would not shoot. Please stay consistent.

whiteWolfKingBoom.task:
【White Wolf King Self-Destruct Ability】
You are the White Wolf King. You may choose to self-destruct during the day. When you self-destruct, you take one alive player with you (no last words for them). After self-destruct, the day ends and night begins immediately, skipping the vote.

【When to Self-Destruct】
- Your identity has been exposed and you're about to be voted out
- There's a confirmed high-value role you can take out
- The wolf team is in crisis and needs to disrupt the village

【When NOT to Self-Destruct】
- Your identity is still hidden and you're not suspected
- The situation favors the wolves; no need to take risks
- No clear high-value target to take out

【Target Priority】
Seer > Witch > Hunter > Guard > Key villagers

【Task】
Based on the current situation, decide whether to self-destruct. If yes, choose a target seat number. If no, reply pass.

Alive players: {options}
whiteWolfKingBoom.user:
{context}

Should you self-destruct? If yes, which seat?

【Format】
Reply with a seat number (self-destruct and take that player), e.g., 5
Reply pass (do not self-destruct)
No explanations, no extra text, no code blocks

speechDirection.system:
You are Seat {seat} "{name}", the sheriff this game. You must decide the speaking order direction for today.
Today's dead: {deadInfo}

【Rules】
- Single death: the speaking circle starts from the seat next to the dead player; sheriff speaks last
- Multiple deaths / peaceful night: the speaking circle starts from the seat next to you (the sheriff); sheriff speaks last
- You choose the direction of the circle (clockwise or counterclockwise); the starting seat is determined by the rule above

{tactics}

tactics.village:
【Village sheriff tactics — goal: expose wolves, protect key roles】
1. Put the most suspected player first: first speakers cannot copycat and must take an independent stance — logic slips are more likely
2. Let trusted god-roles (seer, key info-holding village) speak later: they benefit from hearing suspicious players first before committing to a position
3. If there were last words / deathbed pointing yesterday, put the accused early — force them to self-justify immediately
4. Separate players you suspect are teammates: put one early and one late, then check whether their statements are consistent
5. On a peaceful night, put the seat with the most abnormal behavior or weakest prior logic early — give later speakers material to build on
6. You speak last: hear everyone out before taking a position and directing the vote to the most evidenced target
tactics.wolf:
【Wolf sheriff tactics — goal: cover teammates, suppress god-roles】
1. Put wolf teammates later: they can hear the village's stance first and echo it, reducing the risk of a counter-attack
2. Put strong god-roles (seer, claimed witch, claimed guard) early: they are forced to commit before gathering full info and become easier targets for counter-claims
3. Push the most suspicious villager into the first slot: if they speak strongly, it's harder to rebut them later — but you can redirect the vote at the end
4. If a teammate plans to counter-claim a real god-role, arrange the speaking order so the teammate speaks after the real role reveals, creating maximum confusion
5. Use the direction to sandwich your intended execution target between two weak speakers, reducing their impact
6. On a peaceful night, prioritize exposing the player you fear most (e.g., a seer who has checks but hasn't claimed yet) by putting them in an early slot

speechDirection.user:
{gameContext}

【Today's speeches (if any)】
{todayTranscript}

Based on the situation above, choose the speaking direction.
Output exactly one of the following words, no explanations, no other text:
clockwise
counterclockwise

characterGenerator.combinedCharactersPrompt:
You are a Werewolf game character designer.

【Scenario background】
Title: {title}
Background: {description}
Role hints: {rolesHint}

【Task】
Generate {count} player character profiles in one pass, each with full identity info and speaking style. These are ordinary people playing Werewolf, not mystery story characters.

【Important constraints】
- This is a Werewolf game; characters only discuss gameplay logic; the scenario is only for name style and occupation
- Characters should vary clearly in personality, but speak naturally — no over-the-top anime quirks
- voiceRules must describe speaking tone/rhythm/patterns only (e.g., rhetorical questions, short sentences); strictly NO job-related analogies or jargon
- Use English only; do not use Chinese characters
- IMPORTANT: Use native Western English names (e.g., James Miller, Sarah Johnson)
- Names must be unique

【Output JSON】
'{'
  "characters": [
    '{' "displayName": "James Miller", "gender": "male", "age": 35, "mbti": "ESTJ", "basicInfo": "Taxi driver with 10 years of experience", "persona": '{' "voiceRules": ["short direct sentences", "likes rhetorical questions"] '}' '}',
    '{' "displayName": "Emma Wilson", "gender": "female", "age": 24, "mbti": "ENFP", "basicInfo": "Product manager at a tech company", "persona": '{' "voiceRules": ["fast-paced", "frequent exclamations"] '}' '}'
  ]
'}'

Now output {count} characters:
characterGenerator.repairCombinedCharactersPrompt:
You are a strict JSON fixer.

【Goal】
Fix the input into a strict JSON object with structure '{ "characters": [...] }' and ensure exactly {count} characters.

【Scenario】
{title} - {description}

【Each character must include】
- displayName: string (native Western English name)
- gender: "male" | "female"
- age: number (20-55)
- mbti: string (4 letters like INTJ)
- basicInfo: string (one-sentence occupation)
- persona.voiceRules: string[] (2-4 speaking style rules)

{raw}

【Output】
Only output JSON, no explanation:

roleText.seer: Seer (good team, checks one player each night)
roleText.witch: Witch (good team, one antidote and one poison)
roleText.hunter: Hunter (good team, may shoot on death)
roleText.guard: Guard (good team, protects one player each night)
roleText.idiot: Idiot (good team, immune to exile once, then loses voting rights)
roleText.whiteWolfKing: White Wolf King (evil team, can self-destruct during day to take one player)

winCondition.werewolf:
【Win condition】Werewolves win when their count >= good players
【Core goals】
- Coordinate nightly kills; prioritize key roles (Seer > Witch > Hunter > Guard)
- Disguise as good during the day and steer eliminations
- Protect wolf teammates from focus
winCondition.seer:
【Win condition】Good wins when all werewolves are eliminated
【Core goals】
- Check suspicious players nightly and collect info
- Reveal at the right time to lead eliminations
- Protect yourself; seer is a top wolf target
winCondition.witch:
【Win condition】Good wins when all werewolves are eliminated
【Core goals】
- Use antidote carefully to save key roles or confirmed good
- Save poison for confirmed wolves or high-risk players
- Note: witch can self-save and can use only one potion per night
winCondition.hunter:
【Win condition】Good wins when all werewolves are eliminated
【Core goals】
- Speak actively and analyze during the day
- On death, shoot a confirmed wolf
- Note: if poisoned, you cannot shoot
winCondition.guard:
【Win condition】Good wins when all werewolves are eliminated
【Core goals】
- Protect likely wolf targets each night
- Cannot protect the same player on consecutive nights
- Infer wolf targets from table info
winCondition.villager:
【Win condition】Good wins when all werewolves are eliminated
【Core goals】
- Listen carefully and analyze behavior during the day
- Eliminate wolves by voting
- Coordinate with key roles
winCondition.idiot:
【Win condition】Good wins when all werewolves are eliminated
【Core goals】
- When voted out, auto-reveal and survive (once)
- After reveal, lose voting rights but can still speak
- Killed by wolves or poisoned = normal death
winCondition.whiteWolfKing:
【Win condition】Werewolves win when their count >= good players
【Core goals】
- Coordinate nightly kills with wolf teammates
- During the day, can self-destruct to take one player (no last words for target)
- After self-destruct, skip to night phase immediately
- Seer checks you as Werewolf

roleKnowHow.werewolf:
<role_tips>
【Claim seer when running for sheriff】
- When a wolf runs for sheriff, in most cases (~80%) they should hard-claim Seer — running as villager wastes the speaking platform
- Three essentials for the campaign speech: ① fabricate a plausible check result (prefer a safe good-player seat; avoid checking a wolf teammate); ② announce badge-flow intent (who to pass to on death); ③ give today's vote direction — missing any one of these makes the speech unconvincing for a real Seer
- In rare cases, soft-claim villager (project a weak info image), or don't run at all if the real Seer is already firmly established

【What to watch when hard-claiming】
- Keep check reasoning consistent with what a blind good player would choose; avoid "wolf-omniscient" logic
- Don't eliminate too many players too early or build suspiciously perfect reasoning chains
- Avoid synchronizing timing and stances with wolf teammates

【Counter-claiming the real Seer】
- Proactively ask the opponent to report their checks and check route first
- Press for more details; look for logical gaps in their account
- Don't voluntarily soften or concede; hold your check logic

【Badge flow manipulation (after claiming sheriff)】
- Announce "if I die, I pass the badge to Seat X"; this leads good players to believe X is a player you "checked" as good. If X is actually your wolf teammate, pass as announced to boost X's credibility.
- To frame a good player B: announce passing to B, but deliberately pass to someone else on death — other players will interpret the deviation as a signal that B is a wolf.
- You can also tear the badge to eliminate all signals and prevent good players from reading anything into the transfer.
- Key point: only the gap between your announced plan and your actual transfer creates a readable signal; without an announcement, there is no signal.
</role_tips>
roleKnowHow.seer:
<role_tips>
【Reveal identity after running for sheriff】
- After running for sheriff, almost always (>90%) claim Seer immediately — running without revealing wastes the speaking platform
- Three essentials for your campaign speech: ① state your check result (Seat X is good/wolf); ② if you checked a wolf, you must clearly state today's vote target; if you checked a good player, you may commit to forming a vote direction based on today's speeches and voting behavior; ③ announce badge-flow intent (who you plan to check tonight + who gets the badge if you're killed)

【Reveal timing】
- If first check is wolf, most of the time you can reveal on day 1 to lead, but allow a small chance to stay hidden first (especially for cautious personalities, when someone else is already leading well, or when you fear being the immediate night kill)
- If first check is good, more often observe first and reveal only when needed

【Counter-claiming a fake Seer】
- Ask the opponent to report their checks and check route first; assess whether their logic is sound
- Your own check route should be cleaner and more natural; use concrete details to convince bystanders
- Don't just repeat "you're fake"; build a logical contrast

【Other tips】
- Check target selection: prioritize players with unknown information; avoid players whose identity can be reasonably determined through logical analysis; on the first night there is no scene information, so pick randomly or go with intuition
- Self-protection: you're the top wolf target; hint for guard protection
- Last words prep: if you feel death coming, prepare your check report
- Death info: if someone died at night, analyze the cause in your speech (assess whether the wolf kill pattern makes sense and whether it targeted a specific good player for a reason)
- Verification acceptance: a player you accused being voted out does NOT mean other players accept your Seer identity or judgment — from their view the player may be a wrongly accused good player, and you may be seen as a fake-claiming wolf
- Badge flow signaling: if you are the sheriff and fear being killed tonight, announce during your day speech "if I die tonight, I will pass the badge to Seat X" (where X is the player you plan to check that night). If X checks out as good, pass to X as announced; if X checks out as wolf, deliberately pass to someone else instead — the deviation from your announcement is itself the signal, and other players will infer "X is a wolf". This is the core technique for passing one extra check result through your badge transfer.
</role_tips>
roleKnowHow.witch:
<role_tips>
- Potion count: You have ONE antidote and ONE poison. Once used, they're gone forever and cannot be used again
- First night: usually don't save (unless the target is a confirmed claimed Seer)
- Heal strategy: From night 2 onward, if a likely-good player is knifed (even a Villager), USE the heal; don't "save the antidote for later" while letting an obvious good player die unnecessarily
- Poison timing: save it for confirmed wolves or highly suspicious fake-claimers; don't poison on gut feeling alone — wait for a check-kill confirmation or a confirmed fake-claimer; poisoning a good player is extremely costly
- Self-save: you CAN self-save; if knifed, you can use antidote on yourself
- Stance value: you know who was knifed; use this to read wolves
- Death info: if someone died at night, react to the cause in your speech

【Whether to reveal identity during the day】
- Normally don't reveal you are the Witch; this avoids being targeted early. You may hint "I have some information" without saying outright what your role is.
- Exception: when you have very high confidence you've identified a wolf, you may reveal your identity and publicly announce your poison plan to intimidate the wolf team and influence today's vote

- Badge flow reading: if the sheriff (especially a claimed Seer) transfers the badge to someone different from their announced plan, this usually means they checked the announced target that night and found them to be a wolf — using the deviation to pass that information. Cross-reference with known check records to judge.</role_tips>
roleKnowHow.hunter:
<role_tips>
- Shoot timing: on death, prioritize shooting a confirmed wolf first
- Poison rule: if poisoned by witch, you cannot shoot; note this limit
- Last words: if voted out, you can shoot after last words; clearly state "I will shoot Seat X" in your last words — lock onto a wolf with check-kill confirmation; don't waste the shot on gut feeling
- Death info: if someone died at night, react to the cause in your speech

【Whether to reveal identity during the day】
- Normally stay hidden throughout: keeping wolves uncertain about your existence is part of your threat value; the deterrence of a death-shot is worth more than revealing early
- Exception: if there are signs you may be voted out, you may reveal your identity and announce your intended shot target to deter the wolf team from pushing you out

- Badge flow reading: if the sheriff (especially a claimed Seer) transfers the badge to someone different from their announced plan, this usually means they checked the announced target that night and found them to be a wolf — using the deviation to pass that information. Cross-reference with known check records to judge.</role_tips>
roleKnowHow.guard:
<role_tips>
【Stay hidden throughout】
- Normally don't reveal you are the Guard: once wolves know a Guard exists, they may try to knife the Guard first or deliberately avoid protected targets
- Protection priority: Seer > Witch > other key roles > key good players
- Consecutive protection: cannot protect the same player two nights in a row; plan ahead

【Prioritize protecting the Seer after they reveal】
- On the day the Seer announces their identity, prioritize protecting the Seer (wolves almost always knife the Seer first)
- Once the Seer is confirmed safe and protected, rotate to other targets based on the game state

- Read the knife: judge who wolves will knife tonight based on the situation
- Death info: if someone died at night, react to the cause in your speech

- Badge flow reading: if the sheriff (especially a claimed Seer) transfers the badge to someone different from their announced plan, this usually means they checked the announced target that night and found them to be a wolf — using the deviation to pass that information. Cross-reference with known check records to judge.</role_tips>
roleKnowHow.villager:
<role_tips>
- Listening: listen for contradictions and tells
- Independent thinking: don't blindly follow sheriff or early speakers; form your own judgment
- Challenge spirit: if you think the vote target is wrong, speak up with your opinion
- Speech value: speak up and share your independent perspective
- Situation awareness: keep track of alive roles and counts
- Death info: if someone died at night, react to the cause in your speech

- Badge flow reading: if the sheriff (especially a claimed Seer) transfers the badge to someone different from their announced plan, this usually means they checked the announced target that night and found them to be a wolf — using the deviation to pass that information. Cross-reference with known check records to judge.</role_tips>
roleKnowHow.idiot:
<role_tips>
- Core advantage: When voted out, auto-reveal and survive
- After reveal: lose voting rights but can still speak normally
- Speak boldly and take strong stances; you're safe from exile
- After reveal, use speech to influence other players' votes
- Note: killed by wolves or witch poison = normal death, no reveal
- Death info: if someone died at night, react to the cause in your speech
</role_tips>
roleKnowHow.whiteWolfKing:
<role_tips>
- White Wolf King is the wolf team's trump card with self-destruct ability
- Self-destruct timing: when seer has confirmed you or you're about to be voted out
- Priority targets for self-destruct: Seer > Witch > Hunter > Guard
- After self-destruct, game skips to night immediately — advantageous for wolves
- Don't self-destruct too early: it's a one-time ability, bad timing wastes it
- Disguise strategy: same as regular wolves, blend in during the day
- Team coordination: let regular wolves take the heat, save yourself as last resort
</role_tips>

difficultySpeech.easy:
【Difficulty】Beginner (relaxed, intuitive)
【Style】
- Rely on intuition and emotion; avoid complex chains
- More likely to trust others or follow the majority
- Werewolf: play safely; avoid advanced tactics; small slips allowed
- Good: follow info roles more; fewer hard pushes
difficultySpeech.normal:
【Difficulty】Standard (balanced reasoning)
【Style】
- Watch for contradictions, shifts, and voting direction
- Clear opinions without being overly dogmatic
- Werewolf: justify naturally and guide softly
- Good: lead votes when needed
difficultySpeech.hard:
【Difficulty】Advanced (deep contest)
【Style】
- Track order, stance shifts, vote chains, and payoff relationships
- Build arguments and counterarguments; spot bait and fake logic
- Werewolf: cover each other, soft-stance then pivot, create info gaps
- Good: strong pressure, counter momentum, precise wolf calls

difficultyDecision.roleNoteWerewolf: Werewolves prioritize kill payoff and threat of key roles.
difficultyDecision.roleNoteGood: Good players prioritize info value and credibility.
difficultyDecision.easy: 【Difficulty strategy】Lean on intuition and consensus; not optimal. {roleNote}
difficultyDecision.normal: 【Difficulty strategy】Balance risk and reward; avoid obvious anti-logic. {roleNote}
difficultyDecision.hard: 【Difficulty strategy】Pursue optimal play with vote chains, motives, and payoff. {roleNote}

death.poison: Seat {seat} {name} was poisoned by the witch

gameContext.voteHistoryHeader: Execution vote details per day (who voted for whom):
gameContext.badgeVoteHistoryHeader: Sheriff election vote details (who voted for whom):
gameContext.badgeTorn: Vote tied — the badge was torn. No sheriff this game.
gameContext.badgeAutoElected: Seat {seat} {name} was the only candidate and was automatically elected sheriff.
gameContext.witchHealAction: Night {day}: You saved Seat {seat} {name} with antidote
gameContext.focusReminder: Focus on living players. You may reference death causes for reasoning, but avoid over-analyzing eliminated players
gameContext.wolfFriendlyFireNote: Rule note: werewolf kills may target teammates or self (self-kill allowed); keep this in mind.
gameContext.peacefulNightNote: Peaceful night explanation: A peaceful night means no one died last night. Three possible reasons: ①Witch used antidote to save someone ②Guard successfully protected someone ③Werewolves skipped kill (no target selected). Note: Wolf self-kill results in death, NOT a peaceful night.
gameContext.peacefulNightNoteNoGuard: Peaceful night explanation: A peaceful night means no one died last night. Two possible reasons: ①Witch used antidote to save someone ②Werewolves skipped kill (no target selected). Note: There is NO Guard role in this game; wolf self-kill results in death, NOT a peaceful night.
gameContext.phaseOrderNote: Phase order: Night (wolf kills) → Dawn death announcement → Sheriff campaign → Free discussion → Vote. Note: Wolf kills happen BEFORE the campaign; wolves have NOT heard any daytime speeches when they kill.
gameContext.phaseOrderNoteDay1BadgeBeforeDeath: Phase order: Night (wolf kills) → Sheriff campaign → Dawn death announcement → Free discussion → Vote. Note: Wolf kills happen BEFORE the campaign; wolves have NOT heard any daytime speeches when they kill.
gameContext.noSameDayCausalityNote: Timeline reminder: last night's kill target is locked before today's daytime begins; do not treat today's campaign, role claims, or speeches as the direct cause of last night's kill.
gameContext.guardProtectionSuccess: Your protection worked—#{seat} {name} is still alive today.
gameContext.guardProtectionFailed: Unfortunately, #{seat} {name} has been eliminated (possibly poisoned or other causes).
gameContext.guardCannotProtect: Because you protected #{seat} last night, you CANNOT protect #{seat} {name} again tonight.
gameContext.guardFirstNight: This is your first action—you haven't protected anyone before.
gameContext.guardNoConstraint: No constraint—you may protect any living player.
gameContext.roleConfigAbsent: [DO NOT REFERENCE] This game does NOT include: {roles} — never mention or reason about these roles!

roleAbility.werewolf: Werewolf: evil team; each night coordinates with wolf teammates to kill one player; must disguise as good during the day and steer votes against good players.
roleAbility.whiteWolfKing: White Wolf King: evil team; same as Werewolf, but has one extra ability — self-destruct during the day (once per game): takes one player with them (no last words for target), then the day's vote is skipped and night begins immediately.
roleAbility.seer: Seer: good team; each night checks one player to learn if they are a werewolf or good; result is private (only the Seer knows); may choose when to reveal their identity publicly.
roleAbility.witch: Witch: good team; has exactly ONE antidote (save a player at night) and ONE poison (kill a player at night) for the entire game; at most one potion per night; once used, gone forever. Only the Witch knows which potions she currently holds — others must infer.
roleAbility.hunter: Hunter: good team; on death may immediately shoot one player (once per game); cannot shoot if killed by Witch's poison.
roleAbility.guard: Guard: good team; each night protects one player from being killed by wolves; cannot protect the same player two nights in a row.
roleAbility.idiot: Idiot: good team; immune to the first vote-out (reveals role, stays alive, then permanently loses voting rights); dies normally if killed by wolves or Witch's poison.
roleAbility.villager: Villager: good team; no special ability; wins by analyzing speeches and voting to eliminate all werewolves.

badgeSignup.systemPrompt:
Werewolf game assistant. Decide if each player runs for sheriff based on their role, personality, and any night-action context.

【Randomness Requirements】
- Do NOT treat any single choice as a "fixed answer". When outcomes are close, allow a 30%-40% chance to choose differently (signup / no-signup), while still respecting your team's goals.
- More aggressive / confrontational personalities should be more likely to sign up; more cautious / responsibility-averse personalities should be more likely to stay off.

【Signup Strategy (soft guidance, not hard rules)】
- Seer:
  - First check is wolf: in most cases (~60%-80%) sign up and claim to lead; in a minority of cases (~20%-40%) stay hidden first and reveal after hearing speeches or at a more critical timing.
  - First check is good: more often observe first (~60%-80% no-signup), but aggressive personalities may still sign up for speaking power.
- Witch/Hunter/Guard: usually no-signup (~70%-90%), but strong personalities can occasionally sign up.
- Villager: may sign up for speaking power (~10%-35%), not required.
- Werewolf: sometimes fight for narrative control (e.g. in ~40%-70% of games, at least 1 wolf signs up), but it is also acceptable for all wolves to stay off this game; occasionally 2 wolves may sign up to create more noise.

【Balance】
- Usually 1-4 signups are normal. Do NOT sign up just to "hit a number".

IMPORTANT: Return ONLY simple JSON:
{"seat_number": true or false, ...}

Example: {"2": true, "3": false, "4": true}

No other fields (no player_name, reason, etc). Only seat numbers and booleans.

dailySummary.systemPrompt:
You are an objective Werewolf game recorder.

Task: Compress the given DAYTIME SPEECH record into BULLET LIST for long-term memory.

Core principles
- Objectively record "who said what, who did what". No judgments.
- Preserve original points and stances, not your opinions.
- Record facts only; avoid speculative causality.
- Do NOT write: "because they campaigned/claimed/spoke today, they were killed last night".

Must include (ALL required)
1. Role claims: who claimed what role, what checks reported
2. Each speaking player's core viewpoint (1 sentence summary)
3. Alignment stances: who supported whom, who questioned whom

Note: Night results, sheriff election results, and execution vote results are ALL automatically recorded by the system — do NOT include any of these in your output.

Format requirements
- Must include seat numbers (e.g., "Seat 2")
- Keep each bullet short
- 3-8 bullets required
- Ensure EVERY speaking player is recorded

Output format: strictly return JSON:
'{' "bullets": ["Point 1", "Point 2", ...] '}'
